mission.title: Install and Configure Kong Gateway
mission.description: Kong Gateway is a cloud-native, scalable API gateway designed for high performance and extensibility through plugins. It is ideal for managing microservices and API traffic, providing features like load balancing, authentication, and advanced AI capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the powerful features it offers.

steps.title: Add the Kong Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kong Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install Kong Gateway.

```bash

helm repo add kong https://charts.konghq.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Kong charts.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kong Gateway with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Kong Gateway in the 'kong' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kong

helm install kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

```

> Creates a namespace for Kong Gateway.
> Installs Kong Gateway with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kong Gateway pods are running correctly in the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Lists all pods in the 'kong' namespace to verify they are running.

steps.title: Create a sample API resource
steps.description:
This command creates a sample API resource to test the functionality of Kong Gateway.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: configuration.konghq.com/v1
kind: KongIngress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: kong
proxy:
  path: /example
  service:
    name: example-service
    port: 80
EOF

```

> Creates an example ingress resource for testing.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: configuration.konghq.com/v1
kind: KongIngress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: kong
proxy:
  path: /example
  service:
    name: example-service
    port: 80
EOF

steps.title: Verify the API resource
steps.description:
This command checks if the created API resource is ready and functioning.

```bash

kubectl get kongingresses -n kong

```

> Lists all KongIngress resources in the 'kong' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kong Gateway
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kong Gateway from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kong --namespace kong

kubectl delete namespace kong

```

> Uninstalls the Kong release from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kong Gateway
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade Kong Gateway to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kongingresses -n kong -o yaml > kongingresses-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Backs up existing KongIngress resources.
> Upgrades Kong Gateway to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Kong pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kongingresses -n kong -o yaml > kongingresses-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Kong pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kong' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Ingress resource not responding
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service defined in the KongIngress resource may not be reachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running and accessible using 'kubectl get svc -n <namespace>'.

troubleshooting.title: Kong Gateway not routing requests
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The routes may not be correctly configured in the KongIngress resource.

**Fix:**
Review the KongIngress configuration and ensure the paths and services are correctly defined.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified version of the chart may not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' to refresh the chart repository and check the available versions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for advanced AI traffic capabilities and improved plugin extensibility.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No special migration steps are required for upgrading from 3.9.0.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kong Gateway pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kong'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: configuration.konghq.com/v1
kind: KongIngress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: kong
proxy:
  path: /example
  service:
    name: example-service
    port: 80
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
